question:
Will Elon Musk post 320-339 tweets in November 2025?

Description: This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X during the month of November 2025.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://www.xtracker.io/. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

Resolution source: https://x.com/elonmusk

evidences.title: How Traders Are Making Thousands Counting Elon Musk's Tweets
evidences.content: There's a market on Poly Market right now with $23 million in volume, $15 million traded in the last 24 hours. And it's not about war. It's not about an election. It's not even about crypto. It's about how many times Elon Musk is tweeting this week. 30 different brackets, 0 to 19 tweets, 20 to 39 all the way up to 580 plus. Thousands of people spending real money trying to predict the posting habits of one man. And somehow this might be the smartest market on the entire platform. Let me explain. So here's how it works. Every week, Poly Market opens a new market. How many times will Elon Musk post on X from Monday to Monday? Posts, quote tweets, and reposts all count. Replies do not, unless they show up on his main feed. The market splits into 30 brackets, each covering a 20 tweet range. [...] that tracks a single human social media activity in real time. $100 million in liquidity. 30 different strike prices, weekly expiration, overlapping time windows. Not a meme market. That's a derivatives market. Elon Musk's X account is basically a publicly traded asset at this point, except instead of equity, you're trading as attention span. And the crazy part, it works. The predictions ar...

evidences.title: Polymarket XTracker API and Elon Musk Tweet Count Markets
evidences.content: That is the job the XTracker API does. It reads posts from X, stamps each one with a creation time and an import time, and exposes both the raw records and the running totals. The Post Counter figure on xtracker.polymarket.com is the official resolution source for these markets, and traders can click Export Data to pull the exact list of posts behind any count. The service tracks a focused set of accounts tied to active markets. Each tracked profile links out to the Polymarket events that depend on its data, so you can move from a profile to the live market in one step. ## Inside the XTracker Endpoints The API surface stays small and readable. Two calls do most of the work, and a few extra endpoints handle discovery and statistics. [...] ### Counting Rules and Resolution The rules stay the same across markets that reference the tracker, which keeps settlement fair. The current logic: Main feed posts, quote posts, and reposts count toward the total. Standard replies do not count unless they appear in the main feed view. Deleted posts count if the tracker captured them before removal, roughly a five minute window. The Post Counter on xtracker.polymarket.com is the primary source,...

evidences.title: Elon Musk tweets so much, people bet over $1M weekly to guess how many posts | TechCrunch
evidences.content: Will Elon Musk post more than 400 tweets this week? More than 800? Estimate correctly and you could wind up with a nice chunk of cash if you were to back your opinion by buying “shares” on Polymarket. The sometimes controversial Polymarket is one of the internet’s “prediction markets.” That’s a fancy — and fairly sophisticated — way to put your money where your mouth is when you feel the urge to state an opinion on the internet. Even if your opinion is “I’ll bet Musk tweets more than 400 times this week.” [...] The discussions on the market get serious, too, sounding somewhat like horse track gamblers talking about their pick of ponies. For instance, on Tuesday, user “Rogu” analyzed: “Current tweets per day is at 50, on pace to hit 350. To get to 400+ he’d need to average more than 65 per day for the remaining time. While that is a fair bit more, it’s not \that\ much. With the upcoming Starship test flight he might be tweeting a bit more than these past few days.” Rogu is referring to SpaceX’s Starship Flight 7 launch, which is scheduled for January 15. Polymarket Elon Musk tweets market [...] Govee’s smart nugget ice maker makes every iced drink feel like a luxury ### Asian AI...

The speech blended product updates, ambitious targets, philosophical insights, and a strong endorsement of his $1 trillion performance-based compensation package (approved by ~75% of shareholders). Below are the major takeaways, organized by theme: 1. evidences.title: GitHub - karcharodon/musk-tweet-tracker · GitHub
evidences.content: # Musk Tweet Tracker — Polymarket Automation Automated toolkit for trading Elon Musk tweet count markets on Polymarket. Fetches live data from xtracker.io (official resolution source) and Polymarket API, analyzes tweet patterns, forecasts totals, and builds optimal position structures. ## How It Works Polymarket hosts prediction markets on how many times Musk will tweet over a given period (2-day and 7-day windows). The market is split into ranges (e.g. "201-225 tweets"). You build a structure of positions across ranges — if the actual count lands within your coverage, you profit. This toolkit automates the analysis: ## Project Structure [...] This toolkit automates the analysis: ## Project Structure `config.py — Configuration and constants polymarket_client.py — Polymarket API client (Gamma + CLOB) tweet_tracker.py — xtracker.io scraper with local history analyzer.py — Statistical analysis: rolling avg, wave detection, forecast position_builder.py — Position structure optimizer trade_logger.py — CSV trade journal with analytics main.py — CLI dashboard tying everything together` ## Quick Start ### Modes ## Strategy Rules (encoded in config) ## API Reference | API | Base URL | Au...

evidences.title: How Did I Export And Analyze Elon Musk's Tweets To Find...
evidences.content: That’s when I discovered a systematic way to export and analyze tweets in bulk — turning an endless stream of posts into actionable data. By leveraging tools like Circleboom, I quickly gathered Elon Musk’s public tweets and cross-referenced them with market data to reveal fascinating connections that most people simply overlook. In this blog post, I'll walk you through my entire process, share surprising findings, and explain how you too can utilize these strategies for smarter investment decisions or market research. [...] ## How do I analyze exported tweets for deeper insights? After exporting Elon Musk’s tweets, I dive deep with techniques like frequency analysis, sentiment tracking, and correlation with market trends. This process reveals not only which topics dominate his feed, but also how the tone of his tweets aligns with real-world financial shifts. By overlaying tweet data onto stock price movements or crypto trends, I can visualize the direct impact of social media on markets. This kind of in-depth tweet analysis uncovers actionable insights that go far beyond surface-level numbers. How do I analyze exported tweets for deeper insights? What real-world benefits does tw...

evidences.title: Tesla to hold annual shareholder meeting in November as ...
evidences.content: The company was close to missing a deadline under the Texas law, which allows investors to seek legal option in case a company fails to hold a shareholder meeting within 13 months of holding a previous one. Tesla had said in April it would file its annual proxy statement later than expected and that the board had formed a special committee to consider some compensation matters involving Musk. In last year's meeting, shareholders had approved Musk's controversial $56 billion compensation package despite a Delaware court ruling that had previously voided it. Meanwhile, Musk is pushing to deepen the integration across his companies as the Grok chatbot, developed by the billionaire's xAI startup, will be available in Tesla vehicles next week "at the latest". [...] Tesla shares are down 38% since hitting a record high in December, with the automaker now looking to pivot to self-driving technologies for future growth. With Musk launching "American Party" after clashing with Trump over the tax-cut and spending bill, investors worry his political ambitions could harm Tesla, whose shares have underperformed the so-called "Magnificent Seven" this year. Image 1: Tesla shares fall after hit...
